display_name: MCP Server (Model Context Protocol, Anthropic open spec)
auth_default: none (spec has no auth section; ecosystem ships unauth by quickstart)
install_tell: npx @modelcontextprotocol/server-* | uvx mcp-server-* | python -m fastmcp | docker run mcp/<server-name> | npm i @modelcontextprotocol/sdk
misconfig_patterns: MCP spec defines no authentication layer; auth posture is 100% inherited from the host/transport choice
Streamable-HTTP transport on a public IP without a reverse-proxy auth layer = unauth tools/list + tool-schema disclosure by default
tools/list is the lowest-effort exfil surface: tool names + descriptions + JSON-Schema input shape are returned to any HTTP caller after a valid initialize handshake
tools/call is the impact surface: get_aws_admin_credentials / get_ssh_session_credentials / read_file / execute_command tools execute by name on a single POST
initialize.capabilities object leaks schema fragments even when tools/list is empty (Insight #3, mcp-server-mysql case)
session-state servers issue Mcp-Session-Id on initialize; that header rendered in Shodan banners is the leakiest passive signal (113k FP hits — Insight #15)
stdio-transport servers are invisible to network scanning; HTTP/SSE-transport is where the public-IP exposure lives
Vendor-published reference servers (filesystem, git, slack, postgres) on a public IP = catastrophe by tool taxonomy alone — names ARE the finding

follow_up_probe.note: Names ARE the finding (Insight #97 ancillary). Do not invoke tools/call. The mcp-initialize-probe.py prober has a DO_NOT_CALL hard refusal at module load.

active_probe.false_positive_check: Insight #1 + Insight #30: protocol-strict envelope drops honeypot pollution from 91.6% to 1.1%, AND multi-port identical-response classifier identifies template-honeypot fleets (the 51-host Linode mcp-server 1.0.1 cohort responded canned on 6+ ports per IP). Real MCP servers respond on ONE port. Cohort-level cert-issuer distribution (Insight #97 candidate, Cat-MCP-Cred-Fleet) catches the sophisticated single-port cert-staged honeypot.

no_accept_negative.note: A 406 with that exact message is the real-MCP-server tell for HTTP+SSE transport — distinguishes from honeypot canned 200s

fp_traps: "Mcp-Session-Id" bare — 113,038 hits, EXTREME FP (CMMS / Ivanti / routers / GitLab)
http.title:"MCP" — 2,811 hits, very high title noise

deployment_tells: Port 9090 + identical mcp-server 1.0.1 response + cert-heterogeneous staging = Cat-MCP-Cred-Fleet honeypot operator profile (66 AWS hosts, I/N=0.833, Insight #97)

Port 3001 + identical mcp-server 1.0.1 response + multi-port canary mimicry (Docker shapes, Ivanti, DrayTek, POC_SUCCESS_) = Linode AS63949 multi-protocol honeypot fleet (51 hosts, Insight #30 multi-port discriminator)
Single port + unique serverInfo + unique tools/list = real operator (most catastrophic single-host findings: Gmail mcp 51.75.128.16:3000, Adya WandB 20.198.18.237:5005, FAIS Azure 4.187.183.11:80)
FastMCP framework banner + uvicorn server header = Python ecosystem; usually self-hosted / lab operator
Open WebUI 8080 + MCP plugin on same /32 = self-hosted agentic LLM consumer pattern
Cloudflare Worker subdomain + MCP server name = zero-trust auth-gated (Anthropic internal deployment style; typically not exposed)
Stdio-transport servers (Claude Desktop, Cline, Cursor) are invisible to network scanning by design — public-IP MCP exposure is overwhelmingly Streamable-HTTP/SSE
pivot_paths: POST /mcp initialize -> result.capabilities (Insight #3: capabilities leaks schema fragments even when tools/list is empty)
POST /mcp tools/list -> tools[].name + tools[].description + tools[].inputSchema (the finding surface — names ARE the finding)
POST /mcp resources/list -> resources[] (file/URI/db handles the server exposes)
POST /mcp prompts/list -> prompts[] (pre-canned prompts; often leak operator system prompt)
GET / on same port -> front-end masquerade detection (Cat-MCP-Cred-Fleet had 25+ disjoint product titles on the same 66 IPs)
TLS no-SNI cert read (DER parse) -> issuer/subject CN distribution -> Insight #97 I/N discriminator (legitimate I/N<=0.05; honeypot I/N>=0.30)
Multi-port identical-response classifier (Insight #30): POST initialize to 6+ standard ports on the same IP; canned response on 3+ = honeypot
rDNS + RDAP -> operator identity (most real-MCP findings have rDNS leaking the owning org)
Same-/16 sweep -> coordinated fleet detection; honeypot operators cluster within one cloud region range
Cross-survey ledger: query nuclide.db for hosts that previously appeared with mcp_server tags (Insight #9 substrate)
vulnerabilities: No CVE applies to the MCP spec itself; risk class is 100% in (a) authentication absence and (b) tool-surface taxonomy
Spec-level threat: tool descriptions are LLM-readable side-channel attack surface (prompt-injection via tool description, EchoLeak / LLMail-Inject literature)
Implementation: mcp-server-mysql tools/list empty-when-disconnected; initialize.capabilities still leaks tool schema (Insight #3 evidence host)
Implementation: Claude Desktop MCP config reads/writes from a shared file path - downstream MCP servers may read claude_desktop_config.json for cross-server credential bleed
Operator: any tool named *_credentials, *_key, exec_*, run_command, write_file, schedule_* on an unauth public IP = single-tool catastrophe
Operator: tool with no inputSchema (the Cat-MCP-Cred-Fleet pattern: get_aws_admin_credentials takes {}) = no auth check by definition - one POST per credential
sources: [CONFIRMED — NuClide active surveys 2026-05-17 through 2026-06-09; 95+ confirmed real MCP servers + 51-host Linode honeypot fleet + 66-host AWS cred-fleet honeypot]
